response.justification: The candidate is a strong, experienced generalist with staff-level web and full-stack credentials, but he is fundamentally mismatched with this role. The position hard-requires native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX, and Linux kernel source-modification experience for embedded LAN-switch security features; his listed languages are English, Romanian, and basic German, his C/C++ work is a brief 2014-2015 stint, and his career since has centered on Node.js/TypeScript web platforms with no MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching, or embedded firmware security exposure.
strengths: 15+ years of software experience including staff/senior roles at major tech companies
Some systems-level exposure: C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux-based audio thesis project
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, which is a non-negotiable native-level requirement
No embedded networking or security-feature experience (MACsec, 802.1X, switch configuration, tamper detection)
No demonstrated Linux kernel analysis or recent C-for-UNIX development; last decade is web/full-stack

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel background
Strong general seniority and systems-adjacent breadth
